jokes.text: "Can I get you some coffee?" Wouldn't you love that?
jokes.setup: Waitress offers coffee service
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic commentary on the offer as if it's some luxury
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry cynicism about mundane social interactions. Character-specific but not remarkable structurally.

jokes.text: I don't want Susie yammering about what they are
jokes.setup: Jeff discussing his sexual preferences he doesn't want revealed
jokes.punchline: The word 'yammering' trivializes his deep fear
jokes.explanation: Good word choice comedy - 'yammering' is perfect for reducing serious sexual blackmail to chatter.

jokes.text: The craziest thing I've done: I've been on the bottom. You know, that's it.
jokes.setup: Larry's prudish sexual confession
jokes.punchline: Treating missionary position variation as wildly adventurous
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's neurotic conservatism expressed with deadpan delivery. The contrast between 'craziest' and 'on the bottom' is excellent.

jokes.text: I don't tell my wife anything, I don't confide in my wife. I don't trust anybody. I just treat her like an acquaintance.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his protective strategy
jokes.punchline: Admitting he treats his wife like a stranger
jokes.explanation: Dark and perfectly character-specific. The escalation from not confiding to treating her like an acquaintance is brutal and funny.

jokes.text: It's almost worse than confronting a mourner.
jokes.setup: Larry comparing retrieving Jeff's clothes to other awkward situations
jokes.punchline: Ranking it worse than confronting grieving people
jokes.explanation: Great Larry comparison - his specific hierarchy of social awkwardness. Good callback setup for later mourner references.

jokes.text: Are you thinking about sex?
jokes.setup: Larry being quiet while she reads
jokes.punchline: Her immediate assumption he's thinking about sex
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the 'kinky' theme. Simple but effective misdirection from actual reason for silence.

jokes.text: Whenever you're quiet, you're usually thinking about sex.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's theory about Larry's silence
jokes.punchline: Her pattern recognition about his behavior
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy - the kind of pattern wives notice. Sets up Larry's defensive reactions perfectly.

jokes.text: You got a lot going on in there. Like, sick things going on, is that what you think?
jokes.setup: Cheryl's innocent comment about afternoon sex being kinky
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid escalation to 'sick things'
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic escalation is perfectly in character. Goes from kinky to sick immediately, showing his anxiety.

jokes.text: I've never had a meeting in my house or in my office. I don't even need chairs in front of my desk. Nobody ever sits there.
jokes.setup: Larry's frustration about always traveling to meetings
jokes.punchline: The visual of unused office chairs because no one ever comes to him
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and visual comedy. The empty chairs detail makes it concrete and pathetic. Classic Larry self-pity.

jokes.text: I'd know that tush anywhere.
jokes.setup: Larry recognizing Wanda from behind
jokes.punchline: His awkward way of greeting based on her posterior
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for major plot thread. Larry's oblivious social blindness creates massive future problems with one line.

jokes.text: Have you heard of Columbine? It's idiots like you that cause this whole society to be going crazy with violence.
jokes.setup: Larry playing cowboys and Indians with toy guns
jokes.punchline: Thor connecting children's games to mass violence
jokes.explanation: Absurd overreaction that sets up Thor as the antagonist. The Columbine reference is dark but fits the character's rage.

jokes.text: You are looking at 245 pounds of steel and drop-your-bony-butt-to-the-curb appeal. I will body-slam you so hard that you will poop your bald pants.
jokes.setup: Thor threatening Larry
jokes.punchline: Wrestling persona threats mixed with childish 'poop' language
jokes.explanation: Great character voice - wrestling bravado mixed with surprisingly juvenile language. 'Poop your bald pants' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: I will break it off, and shove it up your sphincter.
jokes.setup: Thor continuing his threats
jokes.punchline: Using clinical 'sphincter' instead of crude language
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Thor using unexpectedly formal language in threats. 'Sphincter' is clinical yet threatening.

jokes.text: Anybody want to help a semi-retarded individual change a tire?
jokes.setup: Larry's increasingly desperate pleas for tire help
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating to the point of offensive language
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's desperation escalating to inappropriate self-description. Characteristic but relies on offensive language for shock.

jokes.text: For a verbal response, $10. Anybody want to make $10 and respond verbally?
jokes.setup: Larry offering money for tire help
jokes.punchline: Offering payment just for someone to acknowledge him
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of desperation - paying for acknowledgment is hilariously pathetic. Great structure building to silence.

jokes.text: The guy's license is '14 THOR.' And he looked like a Norseman of some kind.
jokes.setup: Larry describing his attacker
jokes.punchline: Connecting obvious wrestling license plate to Norse mythology
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy linking license plate to appearance. Larry's detective work is characteristically obvious but stated as insight.

jokes.text: Jesus, am I fat!
jokes.setup: Larry looking at his traffic camera photo
jokes.punchline: His horrified reaction to his own appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple vanity joke but very relatable. The traffic camera catching unflattering photos is universal experience.

jokes.text: I was kind of thinking maybe tomorrow we could do it in my office in Santa Monica... No, that's okay, let's not do that.
jokes.setup: Larry finally suggesting his office
jokes.punchline: Jason's immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the office chair complaint. Shows the cycle continuing despite Larry's attempt to break it.

jokes.text: This is not a meeting about the show. This is about having a meeting.
jokes.setup: Larry treating their argument as the meeting
jokes.punchline: Jason's logical distinction between meeting topics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the absurdity. Perfect economy and structure - the distinction is both logical and hilarious.

jokes.text: Why did you tell Wanda she has a big ass?
jokes.setup: Cheryl confronting Larry about the Wanda incident
jokes.punchline: The complete misinterpretation of his 'tush' comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the tush comment misunderstanding. Shows how Larry's innocent remark became something worse through interpretation.

jokes.text: I yelled out the window and I said, 'I'd know that tush anywhere.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining what he actually said
jokes.punchline: The inherent creepiness of the comment when stated plainly
jokes.explanation: Larry's oblivious repetition of the comment shows he still doesn't understand why it's inappropriate. Good cringe factor.

jokes.text: I like big asses, there's no problem... You like big asses?
jokes.setup: Larry defending his comment about big asses
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's incredulous follow-up question
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Larry digs himself deeper. Cheryl's reaction escalates the awkwardness perfectly.

jokes.text: You yelled out, 'Hey, big ass Wanda.'
jokes.setup: Wanda confronting Larry about his comment
jokes.punchline: Her version is even worse than what he actually said
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how stories get distorted. Wanda's version is worse than reality, creating more problems for Larry.

jokes.text: You have an ass man... You are an ass man.
jokes.setup: The ass conversation continuing
jokes.punchline: Wanda labeling Larry with a fetish category
jokes.explanation: Solidifies the running gag about Larry's supposed fetish. The repetition of 'ass man' makes it stick as a label.

jokes.text: He is such a slimy, spineless creature that he sends you to do his dirty work.
jokes.setup: Susie seeing Larry at the door
jokes.punchline: Her immediate character assassination of Jeff
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie character voice - immediately vicious and accurate. The alliteration of 'slimy, spineless' shows her verbal skills.

jokes.text: Freak That Booty, Big Ass Momma, all his favorites.
jokes.setup: Susie packing Jeff's things
jokes.punchline: Revealing his porn collection with specific embarrassing titles
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the ass theme with specific ridiculous porn titles. 'Big Ass Momma' directly connects to Wanda situation.

jokes.text: He violates me, Larry. He defiles me.
jokes.setup: Susie about to describe her sex life with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Using clinical/legal language for intimate acts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie escalation using dramatic legal language for sexual complaints. Creates maximum discomfort for Larry and audience.

jokes.text: I don't have a closet in my house. I'm used to throwing things. I throw them on the floor, that's how all my clothes are at home... I don't even have a bed. I sleep on a big pile of clothes.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he threw clothes in the trunk
jokes.punchline: Escalating to sleeping on a pile of clothes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing Larry's pathetic living situation. The progression from no closet to sleeping on clothes is perfectly absurd and sad.

jokes.text: I'm a disgusting man.
jokes.setup: After revealing his living situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-awareness delivered with deadpan acceptance. The economy is perfect - just stating the obvious truth.

jokes.text: You like that old car. You know the back seat faces out. So that you can see where you've been.
jokes.setup: Describing the old car's unusual seating
jokes.punchline: The pointless feature of seeing where you've been
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about pointless car features. The absurdity of wanting to see where you've been is nicely understated.

jokes.text: I want you to let the air out of somebody's tires. It says '14 THOR' on the license plate.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Jeff for a favor
jokes.punchline: Revenge plot callback to earlier Thor encounter
jokes.explanation: Good callback and escalation - Larry's petty revenge perfectly in character

jokes.text: Wrestling's fixed... All the matches are set up beforehand. The winners are all pre-determined.
jokes.setup: Larry telling Thor's kids about wrestling
jokes.punchline: Delivering common knowledge as breaking news
jokes.explanation: Larry's petty revenge by ruining the kids' illusion. His delivery of obvious information as revelation is characteristically oblivious.

jokes.text: Dad's kind of a big fake. You know what he is? He's more of an actor than a wrestler.
jokes.setup: Continuing to destroy the kids' wrestling illusions
jokes.punchline: Reframing their dad as a fake and actor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Larry gets more personal and cruel. His petty revenge on Thor through his children is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You tell him the bald turd said hello, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry finishing his revenge on Thor
jokes.punchline: Using Thor's insult as his calling card
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and perfect petty Larry revenge. Using the insult as his signature shows his commitment to the grudge and completes the circle.

jokes.text: We can't seem to agree on the locus equidistant of points.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his meeting location dispute with Jason
jokes.punchline: Using pretentious mathematical language for a simple location problem
jokes.explanation: Great Larry pseudo-intellectualism applying mathematical terms to petty problems. Shows his need to make simple conflicts sound sophisticated.

jokes.text: I suggested a restaurant, he thought it was too close to me. Then he suggested one, and I thought it was too close to him... and, we just can't get it together.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining meeting location problems with Jason
jokes.punchline: The pettiness has reached perfect symmetrical absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing how the meeting pettiness has reached absurd equilibrium. Great structure

jokes.text: Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Cheryl opening mail from the city
jokes.punchline: Her shocked reaction suggests another Larry-caused problem
jokes.explanation: Simple setup for next episode's problems. The reaction suggests Larry's traffic camera photo has caused new complications.
